sections.paragraph:
ἄνομος, ον [ἀ-priv., νόμος] awareness of the close association of the social aspect of lawlessness and the religious aspect of impiety in the Mediterranean world should be maintained when considering the following references

—1. ‘defiant of custom or ordinance’, lawless, wicked

—a. generically, as noun, of pers. 1 Ti 1:9; as adj., of deeds 2 Pt 2:8.

—b. as noun viewed in contrast to avowed compliance with Mosaic law and therefore with suggestion of irony Mk 15:28 v.l.; Lk 22:37; Ac 2:23.

—2. as noun, of a special entity in apocalyptic scenario, ‘epitome of lawlessness’, the lawless one (= ὁ ἀνϑρ τῆς ἀνομίας vs. 3) 2 Th 2:8.

—3. ‘outside the jurisdiction of Mosaic law’, without law 1 Cor 9:21ab followed by qualifying wordplay: μὴ ὢν ἄνομος ϑεοῦ ἀλλ’ ἔννομος Χριστοῦ, not (really) being outside God’s jurisdiction but within the jurisdiction of Christ 9:21c.
